title: azure-rate-limit-dev-run
description:
A new provider-side failure appeared in this window: a dev persona run for story 18 failed at 2026-05-30T03:05:26.666171+00:00 after running for 672.969 seconds. The error text is explicit that the run did not fail on story logic, tests, or prompt parsing; it failed because Azure returned a `RateLimitError` stating the quota request exceeded the token limit. This is a single occurrence in the current bundle, but the diagnosis is clear from the provider error message itself.

The immediate downstream impact is one long dev attempt that consumed over 11 minutes of wall time and did not advance the story. In-window evidence does not show a retry storm, queue skew, or broader tick disruption, so this currently looks localized rather than factory-wide. The same window also shows small worktree drift noted by L1, but that signal is separate and weak here.

This does not appear to be a continuation of the recent prompt-placeholder or missing-template concerns; the observed failure class is different. The most plausible explanation supported by the evidence is an infrastructure/provider quota or token-cap rejection on the Azure model path used by the dev persona. Because the error is self-identifying and attributable to factory infrastructure/provider configuration rather than the story domain, L3 review is warranted even though only one failure is present in this interval.

evidence.summary_excerpt: One long dev run on story 18 failed after attempt 20 with an explicit Azure rate-limit/quota token cap error; no retry cluster formed in-window.

evidence.excerpt: story 18 dev attempt 20 failed after 672.969s: "litellm.RateLimitError: AzureException RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0."

{"noteworthy":"One failed dev run on story 18 ended with an explicit Azure RateLimitError/quota token-limit message after a long 673s run; this is a clear diagnosable infrastructure/provider failure"}
{"noteworthy":"Only one failure for story 18/dev in-window; this is not a retry storm."}
{"noteworthy":"Two on-disk worktrees now point to missing story IDs (52 and 55), up from one in the prior note; small drift, worth watching but not escalation-worthy by itself."}

escalation_reason: Clear provider-side rate-limit/token-cap failure on the dev Azure model path was observed in this interval, causing one 672.969s failed story run with no story-domain diagnosis. L3 judgment is needed now because the error names its own cause, is attributable to factory infrastructure/provider configuration, and waiting for repetition would risk another long failed run.
